channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Bad Bunny’s private audience: Latin trap megastar met Pope Leo in Spain
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola
item.description: Bad Bunny and Pope Leo XIV may have radically different personas, but when it comes to pluralist visions, they are brothers in arms.

item.title: Why rescue donkeys are wearing donated socks from a pro soccer team
item.dc:creator: Kyle Melnick
item.description: Socks from Arsenal Football Club have a new life helping rescued donkeys and horses heal from injuries.

item.title: NATO shoots down drone over Latvia as concern about Ukraine war’s spread grows
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina, David Stern, Ellen Francis
item.description: The drone, which entered Latvia from Russia, was the latest to violate the airspace of a NATO member during the war.

item.title: After escalating strikes, Israel and Iran signal end to attacks
item.dc:creator: Gerry Shih, Lior Soroka, Susannah George
item.description: President Donald Trump had urged restraint after the two countries intensified attacks, and Iran said it would halt its strikes.

item.title: Israelis push Netanyahu to resist U.S. pressure and keep fighting in Lebanon
item.dc:creator: Gerry Shih, Heidi Levine
item.description: As Trump fumes over fighting that has impeded his peace talks with Iran, some Israelis recall the 18-year occupation of Lebanon that began in 1982 and cite “complete déjà vu.”

item.title: Chinese leader Xi Jinping makes rare trip to North Korea in show of unity
item.dc:creator: Michelle Lee, Rudy Lu
item.description: Xi Jinping arrived Monday to pomp and fanfare in Pyongyang, celebrating what he and Kim Jong Un called the “unbreakable” bond between their countries.

item.title: Israel launches strikes on Iran after Tehran fires barrage of missiles
item.dc:creator: Lior Soroka, Sammy Westfall
item.description: The exchange, after Tehran’s first such strike on Israel in two months, threatened to further complicate efforts to broker a peace deal aimed at ending the war.
